steps.title: Use the triangle area formula
steps.content: The area of a triangle is one-half the product of its base and corresponding height. Use $A=\frac{1}{2}bh$ with the given base and height.

steps.title: Recall the triangle area formula
steps.content: The area of a triangle is one-half the product of its base and height:

steps.title: Substitute the given values and calculate
steps.content:
With base $b = 12$ cm and height $h = 64$ cm:
$$A = \frac{1}{2} \cdot 12 \cdot 64$$
$$A = \frac{768}{2} = 384$$
That confirms B is correct.
